meaning: 輕而易舉的事;小菜一碟

examples.en: Don't worry, this test will be a piece of cake.
examples.zh: 別擔心,這個考試易如反掌。

examples.en: Fixing the bike was a piece of cake for him.
examples.zh: 修腳踏車對他來說輕鬆得很。

examples.en: I'm exhausted, I'm gonna hit the sack.
examples.zh: 我累壞了,我要去睡覺了。

examples.en: It's late, let's hit the sack.
examples.zh: 很晚了,我們去睡吧。

examples.en: It's no big deal, I can do it again.
examples.zh: 沒什麼大不了的,我可以再做一次。

examples.en: Losing one game is no big deal.
examples.zh: 輸一場沒什麼大不了的。

examples.en: Break a leg at your audition tonight!
examples.zh: 祝你今晚試鏡順利!

examples.en: You'll do great, break a leg!
examples.zh: 你會表現很棒的,加油!

examples.en: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.
examples.zh: 我今天覺得有點不舒服。

examples.en: She stayed home because she was under the weather.
examples.zh: 她因為身體不適待在家裡。

examples.en: Hang in there, things will get better.
examples.zh: 撐住,情況會好轉的。

examples.en: I know it's hard, but hang in there.
examples.zh: 我知道很難,但堅持下去。

examples.en: We've done enough, let's call it a day.
examples.zh: 我們做夠了,今天就到這吧。

examples.en: After eight hours, they called it a day.
examples.zh: 工作八小時後,他們收工了。

examples.en: Let's make sure we're on the same page.
examples.zh: 讓我們確認一下大家有共識。

examples.en: The whole team is on the same page now.
examples.zh: 整個團隊現在意見一致了。

word: cost an arm and a leg

examples.en: That new phone costs an arm and a leg.
examples.zh: 那支新手機貴得要命。

examples.en: Their wedding cost an arm and a leg.
examples.zh: 他們的婚禮花了一大筆錢。

examples.en: Finals are coming, I need to hit the books.
examples.zh: 期末考快到了,我得用功讀書。

examples.en: He hit the books all weekend.
examples.zh: 他整個週末都在苦讀。

examples.en: Having insurance gives me peace of mind.
examples.zh: 有保險讓我很安心。

examples.en: She just wants some peace of mind.
examples.zh: 她只是想要內心的平靜。

examples.en: Don't give up, you'll get the hang of it.
examples.zh: 別放棄,你會抓到訣竅的。

examples.en: After a week, I got the hang of driving.
examples.zh: 一週後,我掌握了開車的訣竅。

examples.en: We only see each other once in a blue moon.
examples.zh: 我們難得才見一次面。

examples.en: He cooks once in a blue moon.
examples.zh: 他很少下廚。

examples.en: Come on, spill the beans about the party!
examples.zh: 快點,把派對的秘密說出來!

examples.en: She accidentally spilled the beans.
examples.zh: 她不小心說溜了嘴。

examples.en: In a nutshell, we need more time.
examples.zh: 簡而言之,我們需要更多時間。

examples.en: That's the plan, in a nutshell.
examples.zh: 簡單來說,那就是計畫。

examples.en: Can you keep an eye on my bag?
examples.zh: 你可以幫我看一下包包嗎?

examples.en: Keep an eye on the soup so it doesn't burn.
examples.zh: 留意一下湯別燒焦了。

meaning: 敲竹槓;價格太貴的東西

examples.en: Ten dollars for water? What a rip-off!
examples.zh: 一瓶水要十塊?根本是敲竹槓!

examples.en: That tour was a total rip-off.
examples.zh: 那個旅行團根本是坑錢。

examples.en: Oh, my bad, I took your seat.
examples.zh: 喔,我的錯,我坐了你的位子。

examples.en: My bad, I forgot to call you.
examples.zh: 抱歉,是我忘了打給你。

examples.en: No worries, take your time.
examples.zh: 別擔心,慢慢來。

examples.en: You're late? No worries at all.
examples.zh: 你遲到了?完全沒關係。

examples.en: Just chill out, everything's fine.
examples.zh: 放輕鬆,一切都沒事。

examples.en: We chilled out at home all day.
examples.zh: 我們整天在家放鬆。

examples.en: Hang tight, I'll be right back.
examples.zh: 稍等一下,我馬上回來。

examples.en: Hang tight while I check the schedule.
examples.zh: 我查一下時間表,你稍等。

examples.en: It looks hard, but give it a shot.
examples.zh: 看起來很難,但試試看吧。

examples.en: I've never surfed, but I'll give it a shot.
examples.zh: 我沒衝過浪,但我會試試看。

meaning: 重要的事;了不起的事(常反諷)

examples.en: So you won once, big deal.
examples.zh: 你贏了一次,有什麼了不起。

examples.en: Getting the job is a big deal for her.
examples.zh: 得到這份工作對她來說是件大事。

examples.en: Don't decide now, sleep on it.
examples.zh: 現在別決定,考慮一晚吧。

examples.en: I'll sleep on it and tell you tomorrow.
examples.zh: 我考慮一晚,明天告訴你。

examples.en: It's getting late, we should hit the road.
examples.zh: 時間不早了,我們該出發了。

examples.en: Let's hit the road before traffic starts.
examples.zh: 趁塞車前上路吧。

examples.en: Wanna grab a bite after work?
examples.zh: 下班後要不要去吃點東西?

examples.en: Let's grab a bite before the movie.
examples.zh: 看電影前先去吃點東西吧。

examples.en: Hurry up and make up your mind.
examples.zh: 快點,做個決定吧。

examples.en: I can't make up my mind about the color.
examples.zh: 我決定不了要哪個顏色。

examples.en: My room is a total mess right now.
examples.zh: 我的房間現在一團亂。

examples.en: The meeting was a total mess.
examples.zh: 那場會議亂七八糟。

examples.en: Dinner at seven? Sounds good to me.
examples.zh: 七點吃晚餐?聽起來不錯。

examples.en: Sounds good, let's do it.
examples.zh: 好啊,就這麼辦。

meaning: 壓垮駱駝的最後一根稻草;忍無可忍

examples.en: His rude reply was the last straw.
examples.zh: 他無禮的回覆是最後一根稻草。

examples.en: Being late again was the last straw.
examples.zh: 又遲到讓我忍無可忍。

examples.en: Cut it out, that's not funny.
examples.zh: 別鬧了,那不好笑。

examples.en: Kids, cut it out and sit down.
examples.zh: 孩子們,別鬧了,坐下。

examples.en: Try to keep your cool during the interview.
examples.zh: 面試時試著保持冷靜。

examples.en: He kept his cool even when they yelled.
examples.zh: 即使他們大吼,他仍保持冷靜。

examples.en: The exam was a walk in the park.
examples.zh: 那場考試輕鬆得很。

examples.en: For a pro, this job is a walk in the park.
examples.zh: 對專家來說,這工作易如反掌。

examples.en: She's always on the go these days.
examples.zh: 她最近總是忙個不停。

examples.en: I usually eat breakfast on the go.
examples.zh: 我通常邊走邊吃早餐。

examples.en: Text me when you get home.
examples.zh: 到家後傳訊息給我。

examples.en: Just text me the address later.
examples.zh: 晚點把地址傳給我就好。

examples.en: No way, I don't believe you!
examples.zh: 不會吧,我才不信你!

examples.en: No way am I doing that again.
examples.zh: 我絕不會再做那件事。

examples.en: You won the lottery? For real?
examples.zh: 你中樂透了?真的假的?

examples.en: I'm quitting my job, for real this time.
examples.zh: 我要辭職了,這次是認真的。

examples.en: Check it out, this app is amazing!
examples.zh: 看看這個,這個app超棒的!

examples.en: There's a new cafe, let's check it out.
examples.zh: 有間新咖啡店,我們去瞧瞧。

examples.en: On second thought, let's stay home.
examples.zh: 再想想,我們還是待在家吧。

examples.en: On second thought, I'll take the blue one.
examples.zh: 重新考慮後,我要藍色的。

examples.en: Her new boss is a real piece of work.
examples.zh: 她的新老闆真是個難搞的人。

examples.en: That guy is a piece of work, honestly.
examples.zh: 老實說,那傢伙很難搞。

examples.en: Take it easy, don't stress out.
examples.zh: 放輕鬆,別給自己壓力。

examples.en: See you later, take it easy!
examples.zh: 待會見,保重!

examples.en: He got cold feet before the wedding.
examples.zh: 他在婚禮前臨陣退縮了。

examples.en: Don't get cold feet now, you can do it.
examples.zh: 現在別退縮,你做得到。

examples.en: Order whatever you want, my treat.
examples.zh: 想吃什麼儘管點,我請客。

examples.en: Lunch is my treat today.
examples.zh: 今天午餐我請。

examples.en: Pizza or sushi? It's up to you.
examples.zh: 披薩還是壽司?由你決定。

examples.en: Where we go is totally up to you.
examples.zh: 要去哪完全看你。

examples.en: Great job, keep it up!
examples.zh: 做得好,繼續保持!

examples.en: You're improving fast, keep it up.
examples.zh: 你進步很快,繼續加油。

word: the ball is in your court
meaning: 該你做決定了;輪到你了

examples.en: I've done my part, now the ball is in your court.
examples.zh: 我做完我的部分了,現在該你了。

examples.en: The ball is in your court, so decide soon.
examples.zh: 輪到你決定了,快點吧。

examples.en: She bought a brand new car.
examples.zh: 她買了一台全新的車。

examples.en: These shoes are brand new.
examples.zh: 這雙鞋是全新的。

examples.en: No rush, finish whenever you can.
examples.zh: 不急,你什麼時候完成都行。

examples.en: Take your time, there's no rush.
examples.zh: 慢慢來,不用急。

examples.en: We hit it off right away at the party.
examples.zh: 我們在派對上一見如故。

examples.en: They hit it off and became close friends.
examples.zh: 他們很投緣,成了好朋友。

examples.en: Long story short, we missed the train.
examples.zh: 長話短說,我們錯過了火車。

examples.en: Long story short, everything worked out.
examples.zh: 簡單來說,一切都順利解決了。
